title: Dimensionality Reduction Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on PCA, t-SNE, and tackling the Curse of Dimensionality.

questions.question: In the context of Machine Learning models, what does the phrase 'Curse of Dimensionality' refer to?

options.text: The problem where predicting multiple target columns at the exact same time crashes algorithms.

The mathematical phenomenon where adding too many columns (features) to your dataset causes the data points to drift further apart in high-dimensional space, severely decreasing model predictive power and exploding CPU times.
The issue where neural networks 'forget' historical data after training on multi-dimensional images.
A bug in early versions of Scikit-Learn related to 3D Matplotlib graphs.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Unsupervised 'PCA' (Principal Component Analysis) algorithm?

options.text: To classify spam emails into distinct folders.
To artificially inject thousands of random new variables into a dataset to boost variance.

To mathematically compress continuous data by finding a smaller sequence of new 'Principal' vectors that still capture the vast majority of the variance (information) available in the original dimensions.
To query specific columns in large NoSQL databases rapidly.

questions.question: What is a major, famously negative side effect of aggressively compressing hundreds of easily understandable columns via PCA into just 5 'Principal Components'?

options.text: The dataset becomes permanently encrypted.
The output size in Megabytes dramatically increases, filling the hard drive.
The new PCA features become fundamentally unrecognizable mathematical combinations, meaning you completely lose all interpretability regarding *why* the model is making its decisions.
The new features can only be interpreted by a GPU.

questions.question: In the AI landscape, what do engineers typically use the sophisticated non-linear algorithms 't-SNE' or 'UMAP' for primarily?

options.text: Calculating the loss gradients in PyTorch.
Scraping unformatted text data aggressively from the internet.
Compressing ultra-high-dimensional text embeddings (e.g., 512 dimensions or more) down onto a simple 2D plot map solely to visually graph and confirm that distinct clusters physically formed.
To automatically write SQL queries for analytics dashboards.

questions.question: Is Principal Component Analysis (PCA) considered a Supervised or Unsupervised technique?

options.text: Supervised. It requires knowing the target Y labels to calculate accuracy.
Unsupervised. It ignores target labels entirely and simply analyzes mathematical variance present within the X features.
Semi-Supervised. It uses a small batch of labels to start.
Reinforcement. It trains an agent iteratively.
